question.text: A stock exchange member fails to furnish required information to SEBI for 25 days under Section 23A of SCRA. What is the penalty range for this single failure?

answer.text: Minimum Rs. 25 lakh (Rs. 1 lakh per day for 25 days), subject to maximum of Rs. 1 crore
Flat penalty of Rs. 1 lakh regardless of duration
Rs. 25,000 per day, up to Rs. 50 lakh
No penalty unless the failure exceeds 30 days

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23A prescribes a minimum penalty of Rs. 1 lakh extendable to Rs. 1 lakh per day during which failure continues, maximum one crore per failure. For 25 days: 25 × Rs. 1 lakh = Rs. 25 lakh minimum. **The Trap:** The per-day penalty begins from day one. There is no grace period. The Rs. 1 crore maximum is per failure, so multiple failures can result in cumulative penalties. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 25 days of non-compliance = Rs. 25 lakh minimum penalty. This escalation makes timely compliance far cheaper than the alternative.
